questions.text: How is the gradient propagated during the backward pass of a Max Pooling layer?
options: The incoming gradient is routed entirely to the coordinate of the maximum value from the forward pass, and all other coordinates receive a gradient of zero.
The incoming gradient is distributed uniformly across all coordinates in the pooling window.
The incoming gradient is scaled by the learning rate and added to all coordinates.
The incoming gradient is discarded because pooling layers have no learnable parameters.
questions.correct_answer: The incoming gradient is routed entirely to the coordinate of the maximum value from the forward pass, and all other coordinates receive a gradient of zero.
questions.explanation: During the forward pass, Max Pooling selects the maximum element in the local patch. Therefore, only the input cell corresponding to that maximum element contributes to the forward activation and subsequent loss. Gradients flow backward only through the paths that contributed to the output.

questions.text: If a feature map tensor of shape (256, 7, 7) is passed through a Global Average Pooling (GAP) layer followed by a Dense classification layer with 10 output classes, how many learnable parameters (weights and biases) does the classification head contain?
questions.explanation: Global Average Pooling collapses the spatial dimension from (256, 7, 7) to (256, 1, 1). Connecting this directly to a Dense layer with 10 classes requires a weight matrix of shape (10, 256) and a bias vector of shape (10). Total parameters = (10 * 256) + 10 = 2,570 parameters. A flattened representation would have required (256 * 7 * 7) = 12,544 inputs, resulting in (10 * 12,544) + 10 = 125,450 parameters.
